channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🌏 The Other Border Problem: How Russia and China’s Lawfare Threaten the Arctic | War on the Rocks
item.description: What happens when the Arctic starts to look like the South China Sea?Historically, a neutral region where cooperation prevailed, the Arctic is quickly becoming a contested space. This is no more evident than in the increasing scope and volume of Russian and Chinese lawfare…

item.title: 📍 Closing the Gap? Italy Sets New Rules for Its First National Security Strategy Amidst Old Obstacles | War on the Rocks
item.description: Italy may finally be about to publish its first-ever national security strategy, a striking development for the only G7 country that has never had one.For decades, a mix of government instability, competition between institutions, a relatively permissive international…

item.title: 🔐 Congo’s Coltan Belt: Verifying Deadly Landslides At Mines Under M23 Control | Bellingcat
item.description: Since the beginning of 2026, at least four landslides are reported to have killed hundreds of people at the Rubaya mines in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), a major global source of coltan. Coltan is widely used in smartphones, laptops and e-vehicles. With the mines…

item.title: 🌏 Taiwan’s legislature slashes equipment budget, weakening porcupine strategy | ASPI Strategist
item.description: The Taiwanese legislature’s curtailing of a defence acquisition budget on 8 May has impeded the government’s attempts at building a porcupine defence system and has damaged the island’s strategic credibility just as President Donald Trump ...

item.title: 📍 The Fuel Crisis Is Testing ASEAN’s Limits | Foreign Policy
item.description: A coordinated response from the grouping did not emerge from its Cebu summit.

item.title: 📍 The Response to Political Violence in 1968 Was Very Different From Today | Foreign Policy
item.description: Back then, political will existed to meet tragedy with law.

item.title: 🌏 China Is Squandering a Golden Opportunity | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Why Beijing has failed to exploit Trump’s missteps,

item.title: 🌍 Can Trump Get a New Nuclear Deal With Iran? | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Washington has more demands—and Tehran has more leverage.

item.title: 📍 How America Can Coerce the Cartels | Foreign Affairs
item.description: A smarter war on drugs begins with selective punishment.

item.title: 🏛️ US Navy open to building ships overseas, new plan says | Defense News
item.description: The new plan comes after President Donald Trump and previous Navy Secretary John Phelan reportedly disagreed over outsourcing construction on select ships.

item.title: 📍 Don’t give the enemy big, close targets. Move supplies to shore autonomously | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Supply ships are increasingly vulnerable. Navies need numerous small and remotely controlled or autonomous vessels to move material through the most dangerous part of the delivery chain, between large ships held at distance and forces ...

item.title: 🌏 Five ways of looking at the Japanese sinking of a decommissioned Philippine ship | Defense One
item.description: Philippines’ Balikatan exercise saw several missile milestones.

item.title: ⚛️ Trump Class Battleships Will Be Nuclear Powered | The War Zone
item.description: Equipping the Trump class with nuclear propulsion presents benefits, but also massive challenges, and Navy officials had been pushing back against the idea. The post Trump Class Battleships Will Be Nuclear Powered appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Possibility Of Operation To Retrieve Iran’s Enriched Uranium Appears To Rise As Negotiations Sputter (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: Both Trump and Netanyahu made comments in the last 24 hours that point to a possible high-risk operation to snatch Iran's nuclear material. The post Possibility Of Operation To Retrieve Iran’s Enriched Uranium Appears To Rise As Negotiations Sputter (Updated) appeared first on…

item.title: ⚛️ Air Force MQ-9 downs aerial targets with cheap air-to-air missiles | Defense One
item.description: The Reaper demo is part of the service’s quest for cost-effective defense against inexpensive drones.

item.title: 🌏 US, partner nations sink two decommissioned ships during Exercise Balikatan | Defense News
item.description: A two-day strike saw the sinking of two vessels, utilizing land, sea and air platforms from the U.S., Philippine, Japanese and Canadian forces.

item.title: 📍 MQ-9 Reaper Fires Laser-Guided Rockets In Tests | The War Zone
item.description: The MQ-9 successfully tested APKWS rockets against air and ground targets, as the demand for lower-cost counter-drone weapons surges. The post MQ-9 Reaper Fires Laser-Guided Rockets In Tests appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏴 Is Russia the Main Beneficiary of Trump’s Iran Mistake? | Foreign Policy
item.description: The Kremlin is gaining billions in additional oil revenue.

item.title: 🕵️ SOCOM to test SkyFi satellite imagery-to-tablet prototype | Breaking Defense
item.description: SkyFi’s Sovereign Intelligence Platform for US Special Operations Command is designed to allow commanders in the field to task a commercial satellite for near-real time imagery.

item.title: 🏴 EU Takes Aim at Russia’s Abductions of Ukrainian Children | Foreign Policy
item.description: Around 20,500 minors have been kidnapped since war broke out in February 2022.

item.title: 🔐 NDS 2026 – More resourcing for a complex cyber threat environment | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Australia’s 2026 National Defence Strategy (NDS) and Integrated Investment Program (IIP) demonstrate a stronger commitment to cyber defence and capability. They reinforce that cyber for Defence is not just about securing networks but also about ...

item.title: 🌏 Five outcomes that would make Trump’s trip to China a success | Atlantic Council
item.description: Though surprises are always possible, the meetings in Beijing will likely be defined by a narrow set of deliverables. The post Five outcomes that would make Trump’s trip to China a success appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 Saab launches new Carl-Gustaf, air defense munitions, expands production in US, India | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Swedish defense firm has revealed new warheads and provided details on delivery timelines from its new production sites outside Europe.

item.title: 📍 Community trust powers energy growth in the United States | Atlantic Council
item.description: To power rapid economic and technological changes in the US, utilities must strengthen systems with speed, discipline, and trust. The post Community trust powers energy growth in the United States appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 Anti-Drone “Cope Cage” Appears On Russian Patrol Boat | The War Zone
item.description: Ukrainian drone attacks on naval targets are driving the development of new defenses, but overhead screens alone have dwindling efficacy. The post Anti-Drone “Cope Cage” Appears On Russian Patrol Boat appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Navy unveils three new PAEs for aviation, mission systems, munitions | Breaking Defense
item.description: “The establishment of these PAEs today will accelerate acquisition efforts in three key portfolios,” said Jason Potter, who is performing the duties of assistant secretary of the Navy for research and development.

item.title: 🏴 Sensationalism Doesn’t Serve Society | War on the Rocks
item.description: Welcome to The Ukraine Compass, a weekly digest of Ukrainian commentary and analysis from across the political spectrum only for War on the Rocks members. Each Monday, we bring you a curated selection of articles from Ukrainian media offering insight into how Ukrainians…

item.title: 🌍 Kroenig published in Foreign Policy on Iran’s uranium enrichment | Atlantic Council
item.description: On May 11, Atlantic Council vice president and Scowcroft Center senior director Matthew Kroenig published an article in Foreign Policy, arguing that Iran does not have a right to enrich uranium and that Trump should push for zero enrichment in perpetuity as part of any deal with…

item.title: 🛡️ 5 US bases selected for anti-drone pilot program | Defense News
item.description: Two military installations along the southern border are among the five that will receive anti-drone energy systems.

item.title: 🕵️ Turkey’s STM debuts new unmanned systems, is ‘really open’ to Gulf collaboration | Breaking Defense
item.description: “We are already using artificial intelligence, image processing, machine learning and things like that. But we are also going more deep and deep in those areas,” the company’s general manager told Breaking Defense.
